overview.tagline: A premier corporate and residential community at the crossroads of Bergen County
overview.description: Montvale is a prosperous borough in northern Bergen County known for its blend of corporate headquarters, upscale residential neighborhoods, and excellent schools. Home to major companies including Mercedes-Benz USA (formerly) and A&P's legacy, the borough occupies a strategic position at the intersection of the Garden State Parkway and Route 202. Despite its corporate presence, Montvale maintains a family-friendly residential character with well-kept neighborhoods, active recreation programs, and a strong sense of community.
highlights: Excellent public schools sending to Pascack Hills High School
Easy access to Garden State Parkway and Route 202
Huff Pond and extensive recreation facilities
Upscale residential neighborhoods with larger lots

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Bergen County conducts periodic revaluations. Montvale benefits from a strong commercial tax base that helps offset residential tax burden.
realEstate.recentTrends: Montvale's real estate market has been robust, with demand driven by the excellent school district, corporate proximity, and parkway access. Home prices have appreciated steadily. The corporate tax base helps keep residential taxes relatively moderate for Bergen County.
neighborhoodNotes: Chestnut Ridge Road area — Upscale homes on larger lots with a semi-rural feel
Akers Avenue/Spring Valley Road — Established family neighborhoods near schools
Corporate Park area — Newer developments near the office parks
Bear Brook section — Wooded lots with privacy near the Woodcliff Lake border
Summit Avenue corridor — Classic suburban homes with easy parkway access

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided weekly by a contracted hauler for the Borough of Montvale.
details: Trash pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Containers must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Maximum 50 lbs per container with secure lids required
Bulk item pickup scheduled through the DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected on a regular schedule by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastics) collected biweekly
Paper and cardboard collected on alternate weeks
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through November
Recycling drop-off available at the DPW yard
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by United Water (SUEZ/Veolia). Sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Veolia (formerly United Water/SUEZ) provides water service
Sewer maintained by the Borough and Northwest Bergen County Utilities Authority
Water billing is handled by Veolia
Annual water quality reports available from the provider
Emergency water issues: contact Veolia 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Planning Board and Zoning Board review applications monthly
Permit applications available at Borough Hall or online
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of occupancy required before moving into new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough manages commuter parking and municipal lots.
details: Commuter parking available at NJ Transit park-and-ride lots
Permits required for commuter parking — waitlist may apply
Street parking subject to posted restrictions
Overnight parking prohibited on borough streets
Snow emergency parking rules enforced during winter

additionalServices.title: Montvale Free Public Library
additionalServices.description: Community library offering books, programs, and digital resources.
details: Located at 12 DeSomer Drive
Children's programs, teen events, and adult programming
Free Wi-Fi and computer access
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for all ages.
details: Youth sports, swim lessons, and summer camps
Adult fitness classes and senior programs
Huff Pond recreation area with swimming and fishing
Online registration available

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Montvale enforces noise regulations to protect the residential character of neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction noise on Sundays or holidays
Power equipment and leaf blowers restricted to daytime hours
Amplified music prohibited after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: All properties must be kept in good condition per borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and mowed regularly
Properties must be free of debris, junk, and abandoned vehicles
Structures and fences must be kept in good repair
Sidewalks must be cleared of snow within 24 hours
Swimming pools must be fenced and properly maintained

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Pet owners must comply with licensing and leash laws.
keyRules: Dogs must be licensed annually with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be kept on a leash at all times when off owner's property
Pet waste must be cleaned up immediately
Excessive barking is subject to complaints and fines
Maximum number of pets may be limited by ordinance

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking regulations are enforced throughout the borough.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM
Vehicles must be moved during snow emergencies
No parking within 25 feet of stop signs or intersections
Commercial vehicles restricted from parking in residential areas overnight
Violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough registration and safety requirements.
keyRules: Rental units must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of habitability required before occupancy
Properties must meet all building code and safety standards
Landlords responsible for property maintenance
Short-term rentals subject to local zoning restrictions

other.title: Tree Removal
other.summary: Permits may be required for removing certain trees on private property.
keyRules: Permit required to remove trees over a certain diameter
Replacement planting may be required
Tree removal near property lines requires notification of neighbors
Borough Shade Tree Commission oversees tree preservation

publicSchools.name: Memorial Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school for younger students in the borough

publicSchools.name: Fieldstone Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Upper elementary and middle school with strong academics

publicSchools.name: Pascack Hills High School
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school in Montvale serving Montvale, Woodcliff Lake, and River Vale through the Pascack Valley Regional High School District

privateSchools.name: St. Joseph's School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic elementary school affiliated with St. Joseph's Church

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 10 miles

whyMoveHere: Excellent public schools in the Pascack Valley district
Strong commercial tax base helps moderate residential taxes
Convenient access to Garden State Parkway and Route 202
Upscale residential neighborhoods with larger lots
Corporate employers within the borough reduce commute needs

commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus route 165 provides service to Port Authority Bus Terminal. Many residents also drive to nearby Pascack Valley Line train stations in Park Ridge or Woodcliff Lake for rail service to Hoboken. Garden State Parkway provides direct highway access to the George Washington Bridge.

costOfLiving.summary: Montvale is an upper-middle-income community with home prices reflecting the quality of schools and amenities.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Above the state average, consistent with affluent Bergen County suburbs
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Well above the national average, particularly in housing and property taxes

pros: Corporate tax base helps offset residential property taxes
Easy access to major highways (GSP, Route 202)
Quiet, suburban atmosphere with larger lot sizes
Good recreation facilities including Huff Pond
cons: Limited walkable downtown or shopping district
No direct rail service — reliant on bus or driving to nearby stations
Corporate office parks can generate weekday traffic
Higher home prices limit entry-level options
Distance from Bergen County's southern cultural amenities

restaurants.name: Aldo & Gianni Ristorante
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian dining with classic dishes and an elegant atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Teak Restaurant
restaurants.description: Pan-Asian fusion restaurant offering sushi, Thai, and Malaysian cuisine.

restaurants.name: Davinci Ristorante
restaurants.description: Family-friendly Italian restaurant with homemade pastas and wood-fired pizza.

restaurants.name: Paragon Tap & Table
restaurants.description: Gastropub with craft cocktails, local beers, and creative American cuisine.

parks.name: Huff Pond
parks.description: Borough-owned recreation area with swimming, fishing, and picnic facilities open to residents in summer.

parks.name: Memorial Park
parks.description: Central park with sports fields, playground, and walking paths.

parks.name: Bear Brook Park
parks.description: Wooded park with nature trails along Bear Brook, ideal for walking and birdwatching.

events.name: Montvale Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with food, games, live entertainment, and activities for all ages.

events.name: Holiday Celebration and Tree Lighting
events.description: Festive holiday event with tree lighting, visits from Santa, and seasonal refreshments.

events.name: Summer Concert Series
events.description: Free outdoor concerts held at Memorial Park during summer months.

attractions.name: Montvale Corporate Center
attractions.description: Major corporate campus that has housed headquarters of companies like Mercedes-Benz USA and Benjamin Moore.

attractions.name: Historic Montvale Train Station
attractions.description: Preserved historic train station from the Erie Railroad era, a reminder of the borough's rail heritage.

shoppingAreas: Nearby Woodcliff Lake and Park Ridge retail

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 8:00 PM

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
